title: galium boreale subsp. rubioides
intro: Galium boreale subsp. rubioides, also known as Rubi-leaved Wood Sorrel, is a subspecies of the Wood Sorrel plant native to Europe and western Asia. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues due to its high content of tannins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune system function. Galium boreale subsp. rubioides can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal purposes.
